title: Mercado Pago Integration in GoHighLevel — Accept Payments
description: Learn how to integrate Mercado Pago with GoHighLevel to accept payments from Latin American clients. Complete setup guide with best practices.
html_content:
If you're running an agency or selling services to Latin American clients, payment processing just became simpler. Mercado Pago is the region's leading payment platform, and integrating it directly into GoHighLevel means you can accept payments without leaving your CRM. In this guide, I'll walk you through the complete setup process—from connecting your merchant account to processing your first payment. Whether you're looking to expand into new markets or streamline payments for existing clients, GoHighLevel's native integrations make this process straightforward. Let's get started.

📋 In This Guide

  • What is Mercado Pago and Why It Matters for GoHighLevel Users
  • Prerequisites for Mercado Pago Integration
  • Step-by-Step Mercado Pago Integration Setup in GoHighLevel
  • Configuring Payment Settings and Merchant Account Connection
  • Best Practices for Accepting Payments in Latin America
  • Troubleshooting Common Mercado Pago Integration Issues
  • Testing Your Integration Before Going Live

What is Mercado Pago and Why It Matters for GoHighLevel Users

Mercado Pago is Latin America's largest digital payment platform, processing billions in transactions annually across Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, and beyond. If you're selling to clients in these regions, accepting local payment methods isn't a luxury—it's essential. Customers in Latin America expect to pay through Mercado Pago just like North American users expect Stripe or PayPal.

GoHighLevel's integration with Mercado Pago eliminates the friction of using separate payment processors. You can collect payments directly from your sales funnels, landing pages, and client portals without redirecting customers to external gateways. This keeps your conversion rates higher and your customer experience seamless.

For agencies managing multiple client accounts, this integration scales easily. You can accept payments across all your funnels while maintaining centralized reporting in one dashboard.

Prerequisites for Mercado Pago Integration

Before diving into the technical setup, ensure you have these essentials in place:

  • Active Mercado Pago Business Account: Sign up at mercadopago.com with your business information. You'll need tax ID and banking details for verification.
  • GoHighLevel Account: You'll need access to your GHL dashboard with admin or billing permissions.
  • API Credentials: Your Mercado Pago account must be verified and you'll need to generate API keys (Public Key and Access Token) from your settings.
  • Business Verification: Mercado Pago requires identity and business verification. This typically takes 24-72 hours.
  • Banking Information: Link your business bank account to receive payouts from Mercado Pago.

Without these prerequisites completed, the integration won't function. Most delays come from incomplete business verification, so prioritize that first.

Step-by-Step Mercado Pago Integration Setup in GoHighLevel

Step 1: Access Your GoHighLevel Settings

Log into your GoHighLevel account and navigate to Settings → Integrations → Payment Gateways. You'll see a list of available payment processors. Look for Mercado Pago in the list.

Step 2: Select Mercado Pago as Your Payment Gateway

Click on Mercado Pago from the available options. GoHighLevel will prompt you to enter your API credentials. Don't close this window—you'll need it in the next step.

Step 3: Generate Your API Keys from Mercado Pago

Open a new browser tab and log into your Mercado Pago account. Go to Settings → API Keys. You'll see two keys:

  • Public Key: This is safe to share and is used on your public-facing forms.
  • Access Token: This is private and must be kept secure. Never share this token publicly.

Copy both keys—you'll paste them into GoHighLevel.

Step 4: Enter Your Credentials in GoHighLevel

Return to the GoHighLevel integration window. Paste your Public Key in the designated field, then paste your Access Token in the second field. Double-check for any accidental spaces or formatting issues. Click "Connect" or "Save Integration."

Step 5: Verify the Connection

GoHighLevel will test the connection with Mercado Pago. You should see a success message within 30 seconds. If you get an error, verify your keys are correct and that your Mercado Pago account is fully verified.

Configuring Payment Settings and Merchant Account Connection

Once your integration is connected, you need to configure how payments flow through your funnels and forms.

Setting Default Payment Gateway

Navigate to Settings → Billing. You can set Mercado Pago as your default payment gateway for all new payment forms and checkout pages. This saves time if you're building multiple sales funnels.

Configuring Payout Settings

Go to your Mercado Pago account settings and verify your bank account information. This is where your customer payments will be deposited. Mercado Pago typically holds funds for 2-7 business days before releasing them to your bank account.

💡 Pro Tip

Set up Mercado Pago notifications in your account to receive instant alerts when payments are received. This helps you stay on top of cash flow and quickly identify any failed transactions.

Enabling Multiple Currencies

Mercado Pago supports multiple currencies depending on your region. In GoHighLevel, you can set transaction currencies when building individual payment forms. This is crucial if you're selling across different countries—make sure your currency selection matches your customer's location.

Best Practices for Accepting Payments in Latin America

Optimize for Mobile Payments

Latin American users predominantly access the internet via mobile devices. Ensure your checkout pages are mobile-responsive. Mercado Pago's checkout flow is mobile-optimized by default, but your landing pages should be too.

Offer Multiple Payment Methods

Mercado Pago isn't just a wallet—it's a payment ecosystem. When customers check out, they can pay via credit card, debit card, bank transfer, or Mercado Pago balance. Let the platform handle this; don't force one method.

Use Clear Pricing in Local Currency

Display prices in the local currency of your target market (ARS for Argentina, BRL for Brazil, MXN for Mexico, etc.). Currency conversion confusion kills conversions. If you're selling internationally, use GeoIP detection to show the appropriate currency automatically.

Build Trust with Security Badges

Mercado Pago is trusted across Latin America, but new customers might still have concerns. Display security badges on your checkout pages and landing pages. Mention that payments are processed through Mercado Pago, a secure, regulated payment processor.

Troubleshooting Common Mercado Pago Integration Issues

"Invalid API Key" Error

This typically means your Public Key or Access Token is incorrect. Log into Mercado Pago and regenerate your keys. Copy them slowly and paste them directly into GoHighLevel without any extra spaces. Also verify that your Mercado Pago account is in "Production" mode, not "Sandbox" mode.

Payments Not Processing

Check that your Mercado Pago account is fully verified and your bank account information is complete. Unverified accounts have transaction limits. Also confirm the customer's payment method is supported by Mercado Pago in their country.

Webhook Connection Issues

GoHighLevel automatically sets up webhooks for Mercado Pago, but if notifications aren't coming through, verify in Mercado Pago's notification settings that webhooks are enabled for payment confirmations and status changes.

Currency Mismatch Problems

If your payment form currency doesn't match your Mercado Pago account currency, transactions will fail. Make sure they're aligned. If you need to accept multiple currencies, create separate payment forms for each currency.

Testing Your Integration Before Going Live

Never launch with a payment integration you haven't tested. Here's the proper testing workflow:

Test in Sandbox Mode

Mercado Pago provides sandbox (test) credentials separate from production credentials. Use these to test your integration without processing real payments. This is where you discover issues before your customers do.

Process a Small Test Payment

Create a test form with a small amount (like $1 USD equivalent in the local currency). Walk through the entire payment flow. Verify that GoHighLevel confirms the payment and that the customer receives a receipt email.

Check Your Mercado Pago Dashboard

Log into Mercado Pago and confirm the test payment appears in your transaction history. Verify the correct amount, currency, and timestamp are recorded.

Verify Email Notifications

Check that both you and the customer received notification emails. These confirmations are critical for customer experience and your record-keeping.

Switch to Production Credentials

Once testing is complete and successful, return to GoHighLevel Settings and replace your sandbox credentials with your production API keys. Test one more real payment with a small amount to confirm everything works in live mode.
